description: Evaluates session management maturity—service governance, KPIs/KRIs, network and application session enforcement, enterprise SSO and OIDC federation, continuous session validation, and SOC session logging.

capabilities.name: Service exists and is in good condition
capabilities.description: A session management service exists and is reviewed on a regular cadence for efficacy.

capabilities.name: KPIs/KRIs
capabilities.description: Session management KPIs and KRIs—including concurrent session limits, idle timeout compliance, and SSO coverage—are documented and reviewed at least quarterly.
evaluation_focus: Session timeout and idle disconnect compliance

capabilities.name: Network and application session enforcement
capabilities.description: Session-based access for network and applications is enforced centrally with renewal automation, re-authentication, and regular configuration review.

capabilities.name: Enterprise SSO and federation
capabilities.description: SSO, OIDC/SAML federation, SSE, and Kerberos are formally adopted enterprise-wide or for scoped applications and integrated with identity and enforcement technology.
evaluation_focus: Formal adoption of SSO, OIDC, SAML, SSE, or Kerberos
Integration with identity and enforcement technology

capabilities.name: Continuous session validation and risk-based renewal
capabilities.description: Active sessions are validated continuously or on risk signals with step-up re-authentication, token binding, and revocation on posture or context change.
evaluation_focus: Continuous or periodic active session validation
Session revocation on device posture or context change
Token binding or session fixation protections

capabilities.name: Session lifecycle logging
capabilities.description: Session creation, renewal, termination, and anomalous session events are logged securely to the SOC.
evaluation_focus: Documented session lifecycle logging to SOC
Secure session event logging to SOC

evaluation.resiliency_weight_policy: Ask stakeholder for per-capability weights when adding a new evaluation service. This service uses weight 1 for all capabilities.
evaluation.pillar_policy: Some services may have no Technology capabilities; in those cases, do not compute or report a Technology pillar score.
